content:
I'm going to start of by saying this would be very difficult so I understand you probably would not want to support this, but since Roc seems to be trying to focused on raising the bar on developer experience I figured I'd pass the idea along anyway.
I was listening to this podcast and at the 25:24 mark the person being interviewed started talking about supporting keywords in different languages in her programming language, Hedy. So basically the idea is that you don't have to use English at all to program. While this would be challenging, I could see this as a way for Roc to gain popularity quickly especially if the market for non-English programming languages has very little competition. (I have no idea what that market is like as I am a native English speaker) No hard feels if you feel like it's not worth it! :smile:

Hi @John Konecny,
Another podcast listener also brought this up earlier :)
We discussed it here.
Cool! I need to do a better job at searching the posts. :laughing:
No problem, we've discussed a lot over the past years, I don't expect people to comb through all of it every time they want to share something :p
